trials.reason: Book B groups the FilterBar/debouncedFilter fix, the query-params rewrite, and the consumer call-site updates each as adjacent units before moving to tests and lockfile, whereas Book A scatters the trivial consumer $-removal edits before the core debouncedFilter/query-params mechanism that explains why they're needed.

B groups all FilterBar/debouncedFilter changes first (the core debounce fix), then the query-params migration, then consumer call-site updates and tests, whereas A scatters trivial store-unwrap call sites before introducing the debouncedFilter core mechanism that explains them.

Book B groups the FilterBar/debouncedFilter core fix, then the query-params store migration, then all consuming components and tests as adjacent sections, whereas Book A scatters the reactive-store-to-plain-object migration across many unrelated files before ever introducing the debouncedFilter mechanism that motivates it.

metadata.note: single-family judge (Claude): self-preference caveat applies; generator/judge ids must differ where possible
